title: Count whitespace-separated tokens in input
prompt: Read all bytes from standard input. A `word` is a maximal run of non-whitespace bytes; the whitespace bytes are space (0x20), tab (0x09), newline (0x0A), and carriage return (0x0D). Write the total word count as a decimal integer to standard output, followed by exactly one newline. Empty input and all-whitespace input must produce "0\n". Exit with status 0.

input_schema.stdin: <input bytes, possibly empty, up to 100000 bytes>

output_schema.stdout: <decimal word count followed by one newline>

notes_input_convention: Zero reads the input bytes from argv[1] because Zero 0.1.2 has no exposed stdin capability; embedded tabs and newlines inside argv[1] (passed as $'a\tb\nc' from bash) count as whitespace just like stdin reads in the other languages. All other languages read all bytes from stdin until EOF. The harness adapts per language.
